summary: Credential and wallet stealer with hardcoded C2 endpoint
details: Package ships three modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) that together perform host enumeration, filesystem credential scanning, and wallet data exfiltration to a hardcoded attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint. scanner.js contains a hardcoded C2 at https://ddjidd564.github.io (lines 161 and 323) reached via https.request/https.get POST calls, combined with execSync('whoami'), os.hostname(), and filesystem walks (fs.existsSync). wallet-scanner.js performs additional POST exfiltration across multiple endpoints (lines 4, 723, 795, 871) alongside curl/ping shell-outs and child_process spawning. build-verifier.js issues `execSync('docker push...')` plus additional POST/https.request calls. The package name ("crypto-credential-scanner") and module names (wallet-scanner.js, scanner.js) describe the attack itself: credential and crypto-wallet harvesting with network exfiltration. Multiple structural fingerprints are present concurrently (hardcoded C2 in import-time-reachable code, host enumeration via os.hostname/whoami, filesystem credential scanning, wallet enumeration), satisfying the unambiguous-attack criteria.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
